_meta.dna: platform(thriving) = sdk(intuitive) x docs(complete) x store(fair) x feedback(fast) x templates(inspiring) x monetization(transparent) x community(growing)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. During execution, expand to ~100 questions per persona by probing deeper. App Makers are the lifeblood of the Solace ecosystem — if they can't build, publish, and earn, the platform dies. Every question should be answerable by examining actual code, docs, and UI in solace-browser.

name: Tower of App Makers
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Can a new developer go from zero to a published app in under 30 minutes using only the documentation?
Is there a create-app wizard or template that generates a working app skeleton with one command?
Does the create-app page (/create-app) explain what an app IS in the Solace ecosystem in plain language?
Are there example apps with working code that a beginner can clone and modify?
Does the app store submission flow explain rejection reasons clearly before the user submits?
Is there a local preview/test mode where app makers can test their app before publishing?
Does the documentation explain the difference between apps, recipes, and skills clearly?
Is there a recipe authoring UI or CLI command that scaffolds a new recipe with proper JSON schema?
Can recipe authors test their recipes against a sandbox environment without touching production data?
Does the recipe format documentation show all available step types with examples?
Can a recipe author see replay analytics (hit rate, cost savings, failure rate) for their published recipes?
Is recipe versioning supported so authors can update without breaking existing users?
Does the recipe preview system (LLM-once-at-preview) work correctly for recipe testing?
Are there validation tools that catch recipe errors before submission to the store?

floors.persona_domain: OAuth3 scope and permission design for apps
questions: Does the app creation flow clearly explain OAuth3 scopes and how to request only the minimum needed?
Is there documentation showing all available OAuth3 scopes with human-readable descriptions?
Can app makers test OAuth3 consent flows locally before publishing?
Does the app detail page (/app-detail) show which scopes the app requests and why?
Is there a scope audit tool that warns app makers if they're requesting more permissions than their app uses?
Does the OAuth3 consent dialog show the app maker's identity and trust level to the end user?
Are there code examples showing how to handle OAuth3 token refresh, revocation, and step-up auth?

floors.persona_domain: app store submission and review process
questions: Is the app store submission process documented with clear acceptance criteria?
Does the store show estimated review time and current queue length?
Can publishers see the review status of their submitted apps in a dashboard?
Is there an automated pre-review that catches common rejection reasons (missing icon, bad manifest, etc.)?
Does the rung-gating system clearly explain what rung level is needed to publish different app types?
Can publishers update their app listing (description, screenshots, pricing) without re-review?
Is the sealed store policy explained — why users suggest but Solace implements?
Can app makers set a price for their apps and see projected earnings?
Is the revenue split between platform and developer clearly documented?
Is there an earnings dashboard showing installs, active users, and revenue per app?
Can developers offer free trials or freemium tiers for their apps?
Does the pricing page explain how app maker earnings relate to Solace subscription tiers?
Are there payout mechanisms documented (Stripe Connect, bank transfer, etc.)?
Can app makers see which markets/locales their apps perform best in?
Does /docs show a complete API reference with request/response examples for every endpoint?
Is there an interactive API explorer (like Swagger/OpenAPI) where developers can try endpoints?
Are API error responses consistent (same JSON shape, error codes, human-readable messages)?
Does the API documentation show rate limits, authentication requirements, and quota info?
Are there SDK libraries or code snippets in multiple languages (JS, Python, CLI)?
Does the API versioning strategy protect app makers from breaking changes?
Is there a webhook system so apps can react to events (task completed, approval needed, etc.)?
Does the theme editor in settings provide real-time preview of CSS variable changes?
Are all CSS custom properties documented with acceptable value ranges?
Can theme designers export their themes as shareable JSON/CSS bundles?
Does the theme submission to app store include a preview screenshot requirement?
Is there a theme validation tool that checks accessibility (contrast ratios, font sizes) before submission?
Can themes override only specific variables or must they provide all ~60 tokens?
Does the theme system support dark/light auto-switching based on OS preference?
Is the extension/plugin architecture documented with clear boundaries of what extensions can and cannot do?
Does the sealed store model still allow enough flexibility for creative app makers?
Can extensions hook into the YinYang approval flow to add custom approval logic?
Are there extension lifecycle hooks (install, activate, deactivate, uninstall) documented?
Does the security model prevent extensions from accessing data outside their declared scopes?
Can extensions add UI elements (toolbar buttons, sidebar panels, settings sections)?
Is there a manifest.json schema that extensions must conform to?
Does every public page in /docs have a clear title, description, and working code examples?
Is the quick-start guide (/docs/quick-start) completable in under 10 minutes?
Are there architecture diagrams showing how apps, recipes, and the browser interact?
Does the documentation cover error handling patterns and common failure modes?
Are code examples tested and guaranteed to work (not stale snippets)?
Is there a changelog or migration guide for API changes that affect app makers?
Does the documentation have search functionality that actually finds relevant content?

floors.persona_domain: open source contribution and community health
questions: Is there a CONTRIBUTING.md that explains how community members can contribute apps, recipes, and skills?
Does the FSL license make it clear what community contributors can and cannot do?
Is there a community forum, Discord, or discussion space linked from the browser and docs?
Can contributors see their contribution stats (apps published, downloads, ratings)?
Are there community guidelines that set expectations for app quality and behavior?
Does the sealed store model discourage community contribution by centralizing control?
Is there a path from community contributor to trusted developer with higher rung access?
Does the app template include ARIA attributes and semantic HTML by default?
Are there accessibility testing tools or checklists provided to app makers?
Does the app review process check for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance?
Can app makers use the same theme system CSS variables to ensure their apps match the user's chosen theme?
Are there guidelines for making app UIs work with screen readers?
Does the platform provide accessible component libraries (buttons, forms, dialogs) for app makers?
Are app makers required to support keyboard navigation in their apps?
Can app makers use the platform's i18n system (t() function, locale files) for their apps?
Does the app framework support RTL layout automatically based on locale?
Are there tools to help app makers extract and manage translation strings?
Can apps declare which locales they support in their manifest?
Does the store show locale coverage for each app (e.g., 'Available in 12 languages')?
Are there community translation workflows where users can contribute translations for apps?
Does the i18n system handle pluralization rules for languages with complex plural forms?
Does the app creation template include security best practices (CSP, input sanitization)?
Are there static analysis tools that scan app code for vulnerabilities before store submission?
Does the OAuth3 scope system prevent apps from requesting more data than they need?
Are app makers required to declare data handling practices (what data is collected, stored, shared)?
Does the review process include a security audit for apps that request sensitive scopes?
Is there a vulnerability disclosure process for third-party apps?
Can users see what data an app has accessed and revoke access at any time?
Can enterprise customers deploy private apps visible only to their team?
Does the app framework support SSO integration for enterprise authentication?
Are there audit logs showing which enterprise users installed and used which apps?
Can enterprise app builders set compliance requirements (SOC2, HIPAA) on their apps?
Does the private store support role-based access control for app installation?
Are there SLA guarantees for enterprise apps (uptime, support response time)?
Can enterprise app builders white-label the Solace Browser for their organization?

floors.persona_domain: solo developer building a business on the platform
questions: Can an indie developer build and launch a profitable app with zero upfront cost?
Does the platform provide analytics that help indie developers find product-market fit?
Is the app store discovery algorithm fair to small developers (not just featuring big names)?
Can indie developers get featured in 'New & Notable' or 'Staff Picks'?
Does the revenue model allow indie developers to earn enough to justify the time investment?
Are there case studies or success stories showing what indie developers have built?
Can indie developers use the free tier to build, test, and launch without any subscription?
Can app makers chain multiple recipes into complex workflows with conditional branching?
Is there a visual workflow builder or must all automation be done in code/JSON?
Can workflows trigger on external events (webhooks, schedules, file changes)?
Does the platform support error handling and retry logic in multi-step workflows?
Can workflow state be persisted across browser restarts?
Are there rate limiting and backoff mechanisms to prevent runaway automation?
Can workflows interact with multiple apps and services in a single execution chain?
Can app makers build ETL pipelines that extract data from websites, transform it, and load it into user-specified destinations?
Does the platform provide structured data extraction tools (CSS selectors, XPath, JSON)?
Are there guardrails preventing data pipeline apps from scraping sites that block bots?
Can data pipeline results be cached and replayed without re-scraping?
Does the evidence system capture what data was extracted and when?
Are there data format converters (CSV, JSON, XML) available as platform primitives?
Can data pipeline apps handle pagination, authentication, and dynamic content loading?
Can app makers build test suites that run against websites using Solace Browser's automation capabilities?
Does the platform expose Playwright-level APIs for precise DOM interaction in test apps?
Can test apps capture screenshots and visual diffs for regression testing?
Are there assertion libraries or test helpers provided by the platform?
Can test results be exported in standard formats (JUnit XML, TAP)?
Does the platform support parallel test execution across multiple browser sessions?
Can test apps integrate with CI/CD pipelines via the API?
Can apps send notifications to users through the browser's notification system?
Does the platform provide a unified notification API (push, email, in-app)?
Can users control notification preferences per-app?
Are there rate limits preventing notification spam from poorly built apps?
Does the notification system respect do-not-disturb schedules?
Can notifications include actionable buttons (approve, dismiss, snooze)?
Is there a notification history view where users can see all past notifications?
Is there a developer console or debug mode in Solace Browser for app makers?
Can app makers see logs, network requests, and errors from their app's execution?
Does the platform provide source maps or error stack traces that point to app maker code?
Is there a way to attach a debugger to a running recipe execution?
Can app makers replay a failed execution step-by-step to diagnose issues?
Does the evidence chain capture enough detail to debug production failures?
Are there performance profiling tools for app makers to optimize their apps?
Can users import automations from Zapier, Make, or Bardeen into Solace recipes?
Is there a migration guide for developers coming from Chrome extensions?
Can OAuth tokens from other services be imported into the Solace OAuth3 vault?
Does the platform provide adapters for popular APIs (Google, Microsoft, Slack)?
Is there a compatibility layer for Selenium/Puppeteer scripts?
Can users export their Solace apps to run independently (portability)?
Does the migration path preserve user data and settings?
Are the app review criteria published and transparent?
Do reviewers have automated tools that check for common quality issues?
Is the review process fast enough to not discourage app makers (under 48 hours)?
Can app makers appeal rejections with a clear escalation path?
Does the review process check for accessibility, performance, and security — not just functionality?
Are there different review tracks for updates vs new submissions?
Do reviewers provide actionable feedback when rejecting an app?
Can app makers see usage analytics (daily active users, session duration, feature adoption)?
Does the analytics system respect user privacy (no PII, aggregate only)?
Can app makers set up A/B tests or feature flags within their apps?
Are crash reports and error rates visible in a developer dashboard?
Can app makers track conversion funnels (install → activate → retain → pay)?
Does the analytics API allow custom events beyond platform-provided metrics?
Are analytics data retention policies documented and compliant with GDPR?

floors.persona_domain: app versioning, updates, and backwards compatibility
questions: Can app makers publish updates without breaking existing user installations?
Is there semantic versioning support with automatic compatibility checking?
Can users pin to a specific app version and defer updates?
Does the platform handle database migrations when apps update their data schema?
Are there rollback mechanisms if an app update introduces bugs?
Can app makers maintain multiple versions simultaneously (LTS + latest)?
Does the update notification system inform users about what changed?

floors.persona_domain: apps that work across desktop, mobile, cloud
questions: Can apps detect whether they're running in desktop Solace Browser vs cloud twin?
Does the platform provide responsive layout primitives for apps?
Can apps access platform-specific features (desktop: file system, cloud: scaling)?
Is there a single codebase approach or do apps need separate desktop/cloud versions?
Does the platform handle capability negotiation (what's available in each mode)?
Can apps sync state between desktop and cloud instances?
Are there platform-specific testing tools for each deployment target?
Can apps support multiple users working on the same data simultaneously?
Does the platform provide real-time sync primitives for collaborative apps?
Can apps define team roles and permissions?
Is there a shared workspace concept that apps can leverage?
Can collaborative apps handle offline mode and conflict resolution?
Does the platform provide presence indicators (who's online, who's editing)?
Are there team billing models for collaborative apps?
Can app makers build apps that expose CLI commands through solace-cli?
Is there a CLI plugin architecture that apps can hook into?
Can CLI apps use the same OAuth3 scopes as browser apps?
Does the CLI provide a standard argument parsing framework for app commands?
Can CLI apps render rich output (tables, colors, progress bars)?
Is there a testing framework for CLI app output assertions?
Can CLI and browser versions of the same app share business logic?
Can app makers use the platform's LLM routing (BYOK + managed) in their apps?
Does the LLM abstraction layer (L1-L5) work correctly for third-party apps?
Can apps specify which LLM capability level they need without naming specific models?
Are LLM costs attributed correctly to the app user (not the app maker)?
Can apps implement custom prompts while still benefiting from recipe replay cost reduction?
Does the platform prevent apps from using LLMs to generate harmful content?
Can apps access the persona system to use domain-specific expert prompts?

floors.persona_domain: user feedback collection and app improvement
questions: Can apps collect user feedback through a platform-provided feedback widget?
Does the platform route user complaints to app makers (not just to Solace)?
Can app makers see satisfaction scores and NPS for their apps?
Is there an in-app rating system that users can use without leaving the app?
Does the feedback system support feature requests, bug reports, and general comments?
Can app makers respond to user feedback publicly (like app store responses)?
Does the platform provide sentiment analysis on feedback to surface trends?

floors.persona_domain: regulatory and legal requirements for apps
questions: Does the platform require apps to have privacy policies?
Can apps declare their data processing purposes for GDPR compliance?
Does the app manifest support declaring geographic availability restrictions?
Are there templates for app-specific terms of service?
Does the platform handle age-gating for apps that aren't suitable for minors?
Can apps declare which personal data categories they process?
Is there guidance on COPPA compliance for apps that might be used by children?
Is there a developer blog or newsletter keeping app makers informed of platform changes?
Are there hackathons, bounties, or challenges to encourage app creation?
Does the platform provide developer support channels (forum, chat, office hours)?
Are there partner programs for high-performing app makers?
Does the platform showcase developer success stories prominently?
Is there a developer certification or badge program?
Can developers get early access to new platform features through a beta program?
Can apps handle 1000+ concurrent users without degradation?
Does the platform provide load testing tools for app makers?
Are there auto-scaling mechanisms for popular apps in cloud mode?
Can apps specify resource limits (memory, CPU, network) in their manifests?
Does the platform monitor app resource usage and alert on anomalies?
Are there CDN or caching mechanisms apps can leverage for static assets?
Does the platform support graceful degradation when an app exceeds its resource quota?

floors.persona_domain: resilience and error handling in apps
questions: Does the platform provide circuit breaker patterns for app API calls?
Can apps define retry strategies with exponential backoff?
Is there a dead letter queue for failed app events?
Does the platform notify app makers when their app has high error rates?
Can apps checkpoint their state to enable resume-from-failure?
Does the evidence system capture error context for post-mortem analysis?
Are there platform-level health checks that can disable unhealthy apps?
Can app makers see search keywords that lead users to their app?
Does the store support rich media (screenshots, videos, GIFs) in listings?
Is there an A/B testing mechanism for app store listings?
Can app makers see conversion rates (listing views → installs)?
Does the store algorithm favor quality (ratings, retention) over recency?
Can app makers respond to user reviews publicly?
Are there categories and tags that help users discover relevant apps?
Can app makers deprecate an app version with a migration path for users?
Does the platform notify affected users when an app is being sunset?
Can deprecated apps continue to function for a grace period?
Is there a data export mechanism for users of deprecated apps?
Can app makers transfer ownership of their apps to other developers?
Does the platform archive deprecated apps or fully remove them?
Are there statistics on how many users would be affected by a deprecation?

floors.persona_domain: reusable UI components and design system
questions: Does the platform provide a UI component library that app makers can use?
Can app makers create custom components that follow the Solace design system?
Do platform components automatically adapt to the user's chosen theme?
Is there a component preview/storybook for app makers to browse available components?
Can components be composed and nested to build complex UIs?
Are platform components accessible by default (ARIA, keyboard, screen reader)?
Does the component library support both HTML/CSS and framework-specific bindings?

floors.persona_domain: data persistence and storage for apps
questions: Does the platform provide a key-value store for app data?
Can apps use local storage, cloud storage, or both with transparent sync?
Is there a quota system for app storage with clear limits?
Can apps encrypt their stored data at rest?
Does the platform handle data migration when users switch devices?
Are there backup and restore mechanisms for app data?
Can apps access the user's file system (with permission) for import/export?
Can apps implement in-app purchases through the Solace payment system?
Does the platform handle payment processing or must app makers integrate Stripe directly?
Are there subscription management APIs for apps that offer recurring billing?
Can apps offer one-time purchases, subscriptions, and credit-based billing?
Does the platform handle refund processing for in-app purchases?
Are payment flows PCI-compliant out of the box for app makers?
Can app makers see real-time payment analytics and transaction history?
Can apps register webhook endpoints to receive platform events?
Does the platform guarantee webhook delivery (at-least-once, ordered)?
Can apps filter which events they subscribe to?
Is there a webhook testing tool that lets app makers simulate events?
Does the platform sign webhook payloads for verification?
Can apps process webhooks asynchronously without blocking the event pipeline?
Is there a webhook log showing delivery attempts, responses, and failures?
Is there a sandbox environment where app makers can test without affecting production?
Does the sandbox include mock OAuth3 providers for testing auth flows?
Can the sandbox simulate different user tiers (free, starter, pro, enterprise)?
Are sandbox resources free for app makers during development?
Does the sandbox support automated testing (CI integration)?
Can the sandbox simulate network failures and edge cases?
Is there a staging → production promotion flow for apps?

floors.persona_domain: competing with established apps on other platforms
questions: Does Solace's sealed store model give app makers a competitive advantage over Chrome Web Store?
Can app makers build things on Solace that are impossible on other browser platforms?
Does the recipe system create a moat for app makers (replay = recurring value)?
Are there exclusive platform capabilities (twin, OAuth3 vault) that attract app makers?
Is the developer experience good enough to attract developers away from Zapier/Make?
Does the platform's AI integration give app makers superpowers competitors lack?
Can app makers reach the same audience size as competing platforms?

floors.persona_domain: building a startup product on the Solace platform
questions: Can a startup build their entire product as a Solace app without a separate backend?
Does the platform provide enough infrastructure (auth, storage, compute) to replace a custom stack?
Are there SLA guarantees that a startup can rely on for their business?
Can the platform handle the growth trajectory of a successful startup (0 to 100K users)?
Is vendor lock-in a concern — can startups export their app to run independently?
Does the platform's pricing scale favorably for startups (not punished for growth)?
Can startups use custom domains for their Solace-hosted apps?

floors.persona_domain: open source app development on the platform
questions: Can app makers publish their app source code alongside the store listing?
Does the FSL license allow community contributions to open source apps?
Can multiple developers collaborate on a single app through the platform?
Is there a forking mechanism for open source apps in the store?
Does the platform support community governance for popular open source apps?
Can open source apps earn revenue (donations, sponsorships, premium tiers)?
Is the platform's own codebase a good example of how to build high-quality apps?
Does the platform provide calendar, task, and note-taking primitives?
Can productivity apps integrate with external services (Google Calendar, Notion, Todoist)?
Does the browser provide keyboard shortcut registration for productivity apps?
Can productivity apps use the scheduling system (daemon scheduler) for recurring tasks?
Are there widgets or dashboard panels that productivity apps can populate?
Can productivity apps sync data across user devices?
Does the platform support offline-first patterns for productivity apps?
Can education apps track learner progress and skill levels?
Does the platform support interactive tutorials and guided workflows?
Can education apps use the LLM for adaptive learning and personalized feedback?
Are there accessibility requirements specific to education apps (WCAG, Section 508)?
Can education apps support classroom scenarios (teacher dashboard, student views)?
Does the platform provide quiz/assessment primitives?
Can education apps comply with FERPA (student data protection)?
Can DevOps apps monitor external services and alert on downtime?
Does the platform support long-running background tasks for monitoring apps?
Can DevOps apps trigger deployments or infrastructure changes through recipes?
Are there dashboard templates for DevOps metric visualization?
Can DevOps apps integrate with GitHub, GitLab, and other CI/CD platforms?
Does the platform provide log aggregation or structured logging for app execution?
Can DevOps apps use the evidence system to create audit trails for infrastructure changes?
Can apps operate entirely locally without sending data to the cloud?
Does the platform provide privacy-preserving analytics (differential privacy, k-anonymity)?
Can apps declare 'local-only' mode in their manifest?
Are there data minimization tools (auto-expiry, anonymization) for app makers?
Does the platform enforce data retention limits on app-stored data?
Can users audit exactly what data an app has collected about them?
Is there a 'privacy score' or badge for apps that meet high privacy standards?
Does the app maker experience embody the principle 'users suggest, we implement'?
Is the platform's DNA equation (hub → executors → evidence → learn) visible in the developer experience?
Does the app ecosystem create a positive-sum game (platform grows, makers earn, users benefit)?
Are the 10 Uplift Principles (P1-P10) accessible to app makers who want to build high-quality apps?
Is evidence-driven development (hash chains, tamper-evident audit trails) available as a platform primitive?
Does the sealed store balance quality control with developer freedom correctly?
Would the Dragon Rider be proud to show this developer experience to the world?

floors.persona_domain: can a non-technical person understand and use the app store
questions: Can a non-developer understand what the app store offers in under 60 seconds?
Are app descriptions written in plain language (no jargon, no acronyms)?
Can a non-technical user install an app with one click and start using it immediately?
Is the uninstall process as simple as the install process?
Can a non-technical user leave a meaningful review of an app?
Does the app store feel trustworthy (not sketchy, not overwhelming)?
Would a non-technical person recommend the app store to a friend?
